sentence.text: A snare is a sort of trap, or it can be a drum.
I have never heard of anybody throwing a snowball at a snake.
Cameron snatched the snapshot from the desk while the other man's back was turned.
CR
CR
There was a smell of smoke in the house.
The man shouted "Fire", smashed the glass of the alarm and snatched up a bucket of sand to try to smother the flames.
Stars begin to twinkle soon after twilight on a clear night.
"Wriggle and twist and twitch and twirl"--it could be a fish or a dancing girl.
If a thing dwindles, it gets smaller and smaller.
Some quite different words can remind us of painful things; like wrench and wrestle and tweezers and twinge.
CR
CR
CR
The writer had written a letter to his editor.
However, he had forgotten the correct address and so sent it to the wrong place.
CR
CR
CR
The two of them looked out of the window at the snowstorm.
They watched the flakes twirl in the wind and then fall to form a smooth film on the ground.
“Our experiment won’t work if the snow gets much worse."
CR
CR
“Yeah, I know.
We’ll have to expose them again."
CR
CR
“Let’s not worry about that yet.
It may still stop in time."
CR
CR
“Maybe.
At the moment they could be saved by the snowpack."
CR
CR
CR
The two of them looked out of the window at the snowstorm.
They watched the flakes twirl in the wind and then fall to form a smooth film on the ground.
“Our experiment won’t work if the snow gets much worse."
CR
CR
“Yeah, I know.
We’ll have to expose them again."
CR
CR
“Let’s not worry about that yet.
It may still stop in time."
CR
CR
“Maybe.
At the moment they could be saved by the snowpack."
"Instantly" means right away or at once.
If we intend to do something, we have made up our minds about it.
Something invisible is a complete blank, if you see what I mean.
CR
CR
CR
The aim of the treaty was to decide the level of development in the country.
Everything would depend on the delicate timing of the treaty.
CR
The huge impact of engineering on our daily lives is hard to measure.
So what does an engineer do?
CR
We know that it takes an engineer to design engines and machines.
We expect them to work on cars and airplanes.
But we also need engineers to make sure that our power and energy keep going year in and year out.
CR
It is in fact a highly inventive role.
It also needs a great deal of discipline to make sure that the machines they devise don't breakdown.
We want the machines to work all of the time and not just some of the time.
We want to get on an aircraft that lands safely ten times out of ten.
CR
CR
The rock of our well being rests on America's mineral wealth.
We depend on metals and minerals in our homes, schools, hospitals and transport.
We use them to help us to talk to each other and to make our computers.
They are vital for our national defense.
CR
We also need metals and minerals, such as copper, zinc and iron to have a healthy body.
Our bodies use them to build bones, make hormones and maintain our hearts.
CR
CR
The interesting thing is that some of the minerals we need for our bodies to be vigorous, are the same ones we need to keep America strong.
CR
Dave said he wanted to drive the enormous back loader truck to the entrance of the energy plant.
He had big ideas and thought that it would be entertaining to show them who was boss.
Brandon, the truck operator, did not entirely agree with this plan and the discussion got personal.
After searching around the strip mall for Tom, Mia went along to the place they called “The Snakepit".
Finally, she found him there with several of his friends and their motorbikes.
CR
CR
“We’re going to go down the dry gully and up the steep bank on the other side,” said Tom.
“If we take it at the right speed we’ll almost fly over the far edge!
CR
“You’ll never climb that bank, it’s almost vertical!" laughed Mia.
CR
CR
Tom was the first to attempt it.
He started slowly and carefully down the near slope.
Then he opened the throttle and shot up the steep incline.
When he was almost at the top he turned a slow backwards loop, and he and his bike collapsed into the hollow.
CR
They all rushed down to him, fearing the worst.
Tom’s friends grabbed his bike, while Mia ran to where he was lying stretched out looking unconscious.
She lifted his head and cried out, “Tomas, have you hurt yourself?"
CR
CR
He looked up at her and grinned.
“Don’t panic! he said.
“I’m all right.
Where’s my horse?"
If you are thinking about changing colleges, there are many things to research and arrange so that it goes smoothly.
CR
CR
Amos saw that things had changed a lot while he had been away.
There were more cars, new stores and his old school had been closed.
He felt like a stranger in his hometown.
CR
Just over one hundred and fifty years ago, a girl was born in Massachusetts into poverty.
When she was seven, her eyes became infected.
Because they were not treated Anne Sullivan became almost totally blind.
She went to live in the poorhouse when she was ten.
CR
She had never been sent to school and could not read.
Although she could not see she made up her mind that she must learn to read.
She would not surrender to being blind.
She would not give in.
CR
CR
One day someone came to inspect the poorhouse.
Anne flung herself at him and said, "I want to go to school!
CR
CR
CR
Soon after that, she was sent to the Perkins School for the Blind in Watertown.
CR
CR
In 1880 Alexander Graham Bell and others made a device to record sound.
It worked by changing sound waves to electrical impulses, which recorded what was said to it.
CR
In the 1920's, Bell Labs worked out the link between speech sound waves and how a person can tell what they are.
This led them to make a machine which could speak, not just record sound.
It was called the VODER and was shown at the World's Fair in New York in 1939.
CR
CR
This was an early machine that could speak to us.
Now, we have speech software which is used in schools, colleges and homes all the time.
The new sports complex was a very attractive building.
"If you do take this job," said the manager, "I can guarantee that you'll have a busy time."
The wiring was given a thorough check and the circuit was found to be in perfect condition.
CR
Elena and Maria were busy building a disguised coffin for Halloween.
They weren't quite ready yet when they heard a knock on the door.
They opened it and saw a pumpkin, a skeleton and a ghost.
CR
CR
"Boo!" shouted the pumpkin.
CR
CR
"Trick or Treat!" shouted the ghost.
The design of my new phone is so weird; I can't get a signal if I hold it in my left hand.
Aaron recognized his nephew among the boys in the photograph.
The young architect was very proud of the design for the new shopping mall.
Kayla said she intended to resign from her law practice and build a business of her own.
CR
There is a story about a very clever and famous American who once sat on some eggs to see if he could hatch them.
He was disappointed when he found that he couldn’t.
However, at that time he wasn’t yet a famous man – he was just a child.
CR
His name was Thomas, so he was called Tom for short.
CR
Today, we listen to many different devices which have recorded sounds on them.
It’s a common thing to do these days.
But Tom was probably the first person in the world to hear a machine talking to him.
CR
CR
That is because he invented it himself.
This bath is hot.
Get them a map.
Come up this path with me.
I think it is there.
That box is thin and has a lid on it.
They made two separate attempts to climb the mountain.
The IRS agreed that it was a very elaborate scheme which needed investigation.
It was certainly appropriate for the designer of the car to be the first to drive it.
She is a graduate in the Chinese language and an expert on Chinese society.
CR
CR
"I estimate that the river is approximately 200 feet across," said the Homeland Security officer.
CR
CR
"But that is only a rough estimate and it may be wider."
Social conditions in American cities a hundred and fifty years ago were very bad for many people.
If something is confidential, it is meant to be kept a secret.
The word "initial" can mean the first letter of a name or the first step in some sort of activity.
Circumstantial evidence is not supposed to influence the result in a court action.
To advance means to move forward.
An acquaintance is someone who is not quite as close as a friend.
The word "entrance" means a way in, but I have forgotten the word which means the way out.
It is certainly important that an acrobat should have a good sense of balance.
I bet it would be very difficult to train an elephant to dance.
CR
There are four blood groups.
Between 37 and 45 percent of the population have blood group O.
Blood group A is a slightly smaller percentage which ranges from 24 to 33 percent.
Blood group B is 9 to 25 per cent, and only 3 to 7 percent have the rare AB group.
Blood group is determined by the mix of genes you get from each of your parents.
CR
When blood is given out from a blood bank, doctors have to be careful about who gets what.
Groups A and B cannot be given each other's blood.
CR
CR
Group O can safely be given to anyone, and persons having group AB can take blood of any category.
There was an instance of sudden silence in the conference room after the announcement.
Artificial intelligence is the branch of science which tries to get machines and robots to think.
CR
CR
The young Russian was frequently named as the most likely student to win the event.
CR
CR
However, Jayden was apparently full of confidence about his own chances of winning the prize.
Miss Elizabeth Bennett was not comfortable with the way Mr. Wickham trifled with her sister's feelings.
Mr. Darcy felt responsible for the trouble Mr. Wickham caused the Bennett family.
Mr. Wickham's double life was an obstacle to Mr. Darcy's courtship of Miss Bennett.
CR
Miss Mason was very persuasive and tried to get the jury to believe that Lauren had acted with intent to commit the perfect crime.
CR
CR
When Lauren was in the witness box, she said quite calmly that it was perfectly obvious that she had not done it on purpose.
CR
CR
The jurors could not make up their minds and could not reach a verdict.
Miss Mason whistled through her teeth as she left the courtroom.
CR
CR
"She is one cool customer," she said.
CR
CR
At the beginning of the twentieth century, the early film makers moved west.
They were looking for a place to film where they would have control of their own work.
CR
CR
They set up filming first in a small city called Los Angeles, which only had a population of 100,000.
In those days, Los Angeles was surrounded by orange groves.
The film company started scouting for similar locations and found a good site seven miles away through the orange trees.
CR
CR
CR
This particular little place had only a hotel, post-office and a couple of markets.
Its population was 500.
It was called Hollywood.
The Smithsonian Museum in Washington DC is the world's largest museum and research complex.
Canada is a powerful country bordering America.
CR
CR
One of the largest diamonds in the world comes from the USA.
It is called the Uncle Sam diamond and was mined in Arkansas in 1924.
It weighs 40.24 carats.
Wyoming has the lowest population of all fifty states.
The men made a tremendous physical effort to lift the fallen beam.
No one could explain the mystery of the peculiar lights which many people apparently saw in the sky that night.
The two mechanics made a systematic search of the whole area, but the missing cylinder was not located.
That is a bad cut on his hand.
Ben put his leg in the pond and got it wet.
The man is not there yet.
Lend him a hand to get that top off the box.
CR
CR
Tom is fond of his dog Zip.
CR
CR
If Tom mends the hut the wind will not get in.
CR
CR
Then Tom can fix up a bed and Zip can live in the hut.
CR
The hospital official said there had been a slight, but definite, improvement overnight, and the girl was now off the danger list.
CR
CR
Her parents gained the impression that their daughter's condition would continue to improve.
CR
Tyler could not immediately think of the word which meant the opposite of import.
CR
CR
His classmates became impatient, particularly as they thought he should be familiar with the word.
CR
The Land Act of 1785 tried to make the system for giving out land to settlers more orderly.
This helped people know what to expect when they made a claim.
The land to be settled was split up into a 6 mile square called a township.
The township was split into 36 sections, each being 1 square mile or 640 acres exactly.
CR
CR
CR
This new system made homesteading more popular.
As a result, the Homestead Act was signed into law by President Abraham Lincoln on May 20, 1862.
CR
CR
At the club meeting the chairperson declared that everyone knew that UFOs existed.
There were gasps and shouts that he was completely crazy.
The excited members exchanged arguments and insults with each other, but did not reach an agreement about the mystery of these flying objects.
CR
CR
It was not exactly a peaceful meeting.
CR
CR
Finally, the note-taker made a few comments about "common sense" and the contentious subject was dropped.
To be patient and to be a patient are two completely different things.
Everyone needs to do sufficient physical exercise to maintain their health.
Anyone wishing to examine ancient objects would probably go to a museum for that purpose.
Ambitious people are sometimes impatient with others.
The brilliant detective was the only person able to suggest a solution to the mystery.
The two world famous boxers had a mutual respect for each other.
Jacob eventually admitted that he could find nothing to criticize about the new car.
For many years, scientists tried to discover the secret of perpetual motion.
The trainee nurse had to follow the doctor's instructions precisely.
CR
CR
Dr. Sally Ride became the first American woman astronaut in space when she crewed the Space Shuttle Challenger.
She was also the first woman to use a robot arm in space to retrieve a satellite.
Though oil has been used by man for thousands of years, the oil industry is a relatively modern development.
CR
CR
Most people are not aware that oil has been known to man for more than two thousand years.
CR
CR
The Chinese used it for burning, and it was used in the same way in the ancient empire of Persia.
Marco Polo mentioned it in the history of his travels through Persia many centuries later.
CR
CR
In those early periods of time, the oil was obtained from near the surface of the ground.
It was dug out or scooped out for immediate local use.
CR
CR
It was in America, in the eighteen-fifties, that the new story of oil began.
The contours on a map give us information about the natural features of the ground.
A fracture can be something to do with the human body, or with metal or glass.
CR
CR
A thermometer is an instrument to measure temperature, and a barometer is an instrument to measure pressure.
However not all pressure is measured by a barometer.
A man who plays the violin is usually called a musician, but a man who can't play the violin may also be a musician.
Sadly, when there is a riot, we can be sure that there will be violence.
Curiosity drives explorers and scientists to try and find out more about the secrets of the oceans of the world.
Richard the Lionheart was a famous English warrior king.
The pioneers of the American frontier gradually worked their way westward.
It has often been said that the political and democratic ideas of ancient Greece have been copied in many parts of the world.
CR
It is hard for us to imagine the lack of domestic comfort when traveling across America in a covered wagon in the nineteenth century.
CR
CR
The growth of the country's economic wealth, road traffic and, of course, domestic white goods since then has been astonishing.
CR
CR
Someone, ages ago, must have been the first person to think of using rollers to move heavy loads.
It could be argued that this person gave the first clue to the invention of ball bearings.
CR
The principle of ball bearings is quite simple.
They are made of extremely hard steel.
They run in a hardened steel channel between the moving part and the rigid part of, for instance, a wheel.
The hub, or center-piece, of the wheel revolves freely around it.
Without ball bearings, such a thing as a bicycle wheel would not be nearly as efficient as it is.
The sand is hot!
Get a bottle of soda.
Have a little fun with it.
Put a cup of milk in it and mix it up.
There was a considerable amount of conversation on the subject, but no conclusion was reached.
In a struggle, one side attempts to subdue or conquer the other and make them surrender or submit.
Plastic is a substance which has become a substitute for many things, such as wood, metal or glass.
CR
CR
A submarine is a boat which can submerge below the surface of the sea.
The US Navy has two kinds: attack submarines and ballistic missile submarines.
Many of these are longer than a football field.
The practice of astrology has gone on throughout the world for centuries.
Psychology is the scientific study of the human mind and its functions, especially of human behavior in context.
After the disturbance which spoiled the football game, the referee was pleased when he received an apology from the team coach.
Two well known practical branches of science are geology and biology.
When the fog rolls in off the sound, it is advisable not to accelerate but to slow down in case of accidents.
Rockefeller once said, "If you want to succeed, you should strike out on new paths rather than travel the worn paths of accepted success."
The technical expert had to accept the outcome of the experiment.
When the nation was anxious about its future, President Roosevelt said, "We have nothing to fear but fear itself."
Through the opaque glass in the door, he saw the vague outline of a woman.
There was a picturesque view looking out over the valley from the kitchen window.
People may be able to buy a unique work of art at an antique fair.
If they prefer modern art they could look through the catalog of a local art gallery.
The professor was a charming, but vague, character.
Electric fuses are meant to provide protection to the circuit and, in that way, help to prevent accidents.
The house was advertised as a unique property, which was sure to increase rapidly in value.
The production manager said he could promise that the new process would produce a profit within the next twelve months.
Ice cream parlors are still popular places to meet friends and eat lots of different flavors.
Which flavor do you like best?
The doctor prescribed just rest and quiet, with no special medicine.
It was a bright sunny morning, in contrast to the previous day.
The climbers made careful plans and completed all their preparations with plenty of time to spare.
"Pride and Prejudice" is a famous novel by Jane Austen and has been adapted for film and TV.
Before going off on vacation, it is wise to take the precaution of locking all your doors and windows.
Positive and negative are opposite words which sometimes mean the same as plus and minus.
The foreign representative put forward a tentative suggestion on how to break the tension between the two countries.
Some of the most imaginative writers and composers have been quiet people, not at all demonstrative as might have been expected from their work.
There was a period of comparative calm after the earthquake before a series of aftershocks shook the city.
The most creative scientists are often the most imaginative thinkers.
CR
CR
Hovenweep National Monument is located on land in south-western Colorado and south-eastern Utah.
CR
Hovenweep is a tribal word meaning "Deserted Valley".
The earliest human buildings at Hovenweep date to over 10,000 years ago when nomadic peoples went to that area to gather food and hunt animals.
CR
CR
More recent buildings include towers which were built by a farming culture from about 500 A.D. to 1300 A.D.
Most of the structures at Hovenweep were built between 1200 and 1300 A.D.
There is a mix of shapes and sizes, including square and circular towers.
CR
The slots and doors of one of the towers have been shown to make a calendar that runs on the sun's orbit.
The building is lined up so that light shines precisely through openings into the building at the sunset of the summer solstice, the winter solstice and the spring and fall equinox.
The light shines in a predictable pattern on the door frames inside the building.
CR
CR
CR
People have long wondered what Hovenweep was created for, but its true function remains a mystery.
Greg made up his mind to prepare a special birthday surprise for his young sister, Katherine.
The deeper you submerge under water, the greater the pressure, and the higher you ascend up a mountain, the lower the temperature.
During the period of the American Civil War, the notorious General Custer had been one of the youngest colonels in the Union Cavalry.
It is fascinating to study the annual phenomenon of bird migration and discover the remote destinations to which the birds return.
The survival of the human race may depend on successful scientific research into revolutionary new medicines.
The designer of the new tire claimed that the process of production in the factory was quite unique.
Dry the bottle and put it there.
Try a little bit of this.
Pass by the pond to the end of the path.
Get a dry rag and mop up the mess.
The top of the bottle is loose.
CR
CR
Bill taps it with his foot and soon there is a mess.
CR
CR
There is a big pool of soda in the room.
CR
CR
Bill puts his big feet in the soda.
CR
CR
His mom tells him that is not cool.
“You can do it, Little Red Caboose."
CR
CR
“I will do it if you let me try."
CR
CR
“Come on, we shall lose it soon."
CR
CR
"Will it pull me up?
It is steep there."
CR
CR
“Yes it will, if you toot as a warning."
Ben took a thin cut of wood to his dad.
CR
His dad had a gap to fix in the hut wall.
CR
The bit of wood was OK for the job.
CR
CR
"Look in the book and see if it tells us what to do, Dad."
CR
CR
"That was a good thing to think of, Ben."
CR
CR
Poor Ben hit his hand.
He had some bad luck there.
It's a big thing with tusks on it.
A desk is a thing you can put a laptop on.
If you bask in the hot sun you can get ill.
Swim in a mask and you can see all the fish.
Dusk is at the end of the day.
It is a very long way to go.
Can anything be done to help him?
Don't worry, it will come off in the end.
Can you carry this for me?
CR
CR
Mike is not going to do it.
Look, he will very soon fall off the tree.
Try to stop him if you can.
Give him a warning, while I go and get some help.
I hope he will not risk trying to get up to the top.
Here are some men with a rope.
Don't let go, Mike.
In a little while you will be OK.
CR
CR
“That was not a very funny joke."
CR
CR
“Well, I did not mean to do any harm.
It was just a lark—for fun."
CR
CR
“Look at this tar.
Can you get it off?"
CR
CR
“Yes, I can cut it off with a..."
CR
CR
“No you can’t.
You have made a big mess as it is.
I do not want you to do more."
CR
CR
My boss is coming to dinner with his wife.
CR
I can cook a fine meal for them.
CR
But I always want to sing when I worry!
CR
That may not be a good thing if I want to do well in my job.
CR
CR
I am going to the park to play ball.
My dog is good at getting the balls for me when I hit them.
He can dart and dive with the game.
At the end, he likes to race me back to the car.
Then we go for a hot dog and a drink.
He can get a bone.
It is always fun to play ball with my dog.
CR
CR
Don’t let the dog out!
CR
If that happens he will run away.
CR
I can remember all the other times he was lost.
CR
There’s broken glass by the river and I don’t want him to cut himself.
CR
CR
Tom sees a nest of beetles in the dust, which he does not like.
He gets a rake to cluster the pests in a pile.
He can play a trick on his sister and take them to her, just so he can see her run fast.
He likes playing tricks on her and she detests bugs.
It will annoy her, but it will amuse him.
CR
CR
The old clock on the dusty shelf began to tick faster and faster.
Before long the hands were still.
Jen had a look at it, but it was broken.
She had to admit it was beyond fixing as well.
CR
“Please take a seat.
Carla Brown will be with you soon."
CR
CR
Emma did as she was told.
CR
She looked at the clock.
CR
She looked at the door.
CR
She looked at her tablet and began to relax.
CR
At last, she forgot why she was there.
CR
“Well, hi there, Emma.
Let’s get going with this front crown, shall we?"
CR
CR
Wow, it all came back!
Carla Brown was the dentist, and she was next.
Try to make it flatter so it fits in the trunk.
Use a plank of wood to cross the beaver dam.
The story begins with, "Once upon a time".
The best player is the one who wins the prize.
Pick a spot in the shade away from the sun.
The ship hit a rock while it was going at full speed.
With luck we can get home before the others.
Stock up with food from the store while we are here.
CR
CR
It was a crisp and frosty morning.
Kate was jumping about and stamping her feet to try to get warm.
Ella got some sticks to make a fire.
The sticks were damp so the fire went out.
CR
CR
At last Ella had the fire lit and she put a pan full of water on it.
Then Kate looked to see what there was to eat.
Soon eggs and bacon gave a great start to the day.
CR
It's hard to make it in sport.
You need to run fast, shoot hoops and hit and catch lots of balls.
You will get a lot of help if you can get on a team.
CR
CR
Once you know your best sport, there is even more to do to be a winner.
But the best feeling of all is when you get to the top.
Let's read some upside down things.
When I have two planks of wood I can join them with a saw.
My lawn has nice red grass all over it.
A hawk is a little blue bird and it sings outside my house every morning.
All the birds stop singing at dawn.
Cook the meat in the oven to make it raw.
CR
CR
It was after 10 o’clock and it was very dark.
Jacob was feeling sleepy in the back seat of the car.
Suddenly, there was a loud bang.
The car bumped from side to side.
Then at last it came to a stop.
Jacob’s dad put the light on.
CR
CR
"Hey Jacob, we’ll have to see what kind of a mess that has made of our back tire."
CR
CR
Jacob saw that his dad wasn't happy.
They got out to see what had happened.
You can find a trunk on a tree, but also on a car.
There was a loud cheer for the winning song.
If you say you can bank on something, it means it is safe.
It must be Sunday, as the bells are ringing in church.
CR
CR
Five hundred years ago, America was unknown to the world.
Now, it is the richest and strongest place of our time.
Back then, all sea travel was in ships with sails.
There were no cars and there was no industry.
CR
Electric power and gas were unknown.
CR
“Make the knot as tight as you can.
This rock face must be a good eighty feet down.
CR
CR
Watch out for loose stones when you start going down.
If you feel you are slipping, shout and hang on tight.
I’m going to be very happy when you are safe and all right at the bottom."
CR
CR
CR
“You’ll be happy?
What do you think I feel like?
I’ve never done this sort of thing before.
I might even have a broken leg by the time I get down there."
CR
My watch is slow, so I don’t know what the right time is.
I know, I’ll have a look at the alarm clock in the bedroom.
CR
CR
Oh no!
Is that what time it is?
The morning is just about gone.
I have to get going, as I’ve got so much left to do.
CR
CR
CR
Oh, shoot, I had forgotten I’d planned to go out with Jaylen.
There’s her car now.
This stuff will have to be fixed when I get back.
CR
The Declaration of Independence was voted for by Congress on July 4, 1776.
The wise men who wrote it said that they opposed the way in which they had been ruled by Britain.
They knew the taxes imposed on them were unfair.
The people rose up against the King.
This paper lit the fuse of the War of Independence.
CR
CR
Try to get the car to turn the corner without hitting the curb and bursting a tire.
We want to be careful, so that we don’t drive into that big hole.
When it says “Beware—road closed” it means just that.
CR
CR
We have to watch out for more road work.
Don’t scare me by trying to drive too fast.
Look at the speed that car is going!
It is just a yellow blur.
Oh, no wonder.
It’s a taxi.
CR
CR
A tall tale is far-fetched but tells how something came to be.
Long ago, the first settlers told tall tales for fun.
One set of tall tales comes from lumberjacks.
These are the men who cut down trees and then sent the logs down-river as rafts and slept in camps.
CR
CR
The old time lumberjacks were from Maine, the Great Lakes, Minnesota and French Canada.
They were the best in their trade and they went from woods to woods as a job and as a way of life.
They will tell you of Paul Bunyan.
CR
CR
CR
There are many tales about him.
Each camp had its own set of tales.
The lumberjacks went from camp to camp, sharing these yarns on long winter nights.
CR
If we regret something it means that we feel sadness over something that has happened or that has been done.
Sometimes, a thing which we regret is something which we have done to upset someone else.
Regrets can be kept to ourselves, so the person we have upset may never know how sorry we are.
But it is better to say sorry.
CR
CR
The slab of stone was too big for Max to move by himself.
He tried to pull it to one side.
Ethan came over to help him and together they made it slide sideways.
CR
CR
Then, all at once, the stone slipped, slid over the rocks and crashed down the slope.
Max looked into the hole and then turned to Ethan to ask him what he thought.
CR
CR
“What if it’s a place where somebody hid something a long time ago?" said Ethan.
CR
CR
“Don’t be a nut,” said Max.
CR
“It’s nothing but a hole in the side of the hill."
CR
CR
Many cab firms in the US have cars which are yellow.
But why such a bright color?
The boss of the Yellow Cab Company set up his firm in Chicago in 1915.
His name was John Hertz.
CR
CR
Hertz read a study, that said the color yellow, with a bit of red mixed in, was easy to see from far away.
He painted all his taxi-cabs this color, which became known as "taxi-yellow".
Other cab firms did the same, but Hertz was the first.
CR
CR
CR
We can see the glowing yellow cabs in front of hotels and the views of New York would not be quite the same without them.
CR
Playing with a sled is a lot of fun at any age.
Watch out for rocks, because you have to dodge them.
CR
If you don't judge things right, you will have an awesome crash.
As the sled goes one way, you go the other and end up eating snow.
Others are watching and will think it really funny if you take a dive.
So be careful when you set off on a sled ride.
When we say something is huge, we mean it is very large.
If a car is damaged in a crash, it has to be taken to a garage to be fixed.
In the past, a soldier in the field of battle had a shield to protect him.
A badge is something which a person wears to show what he stands for.
You can wind up a clock or a toy, but a wind can be cold.
A bright star is something that sparkles.
You consult someone when you respect their view.
When we say we take something, "with a pinch of salt", it means we do not quite trust what is being said.
The quilt has a fault as a result of the backing not being flat.
Tickle a fish and you can catch it, but tickle a baby and she just chuckles at you.
CR
CR
Super Storm Sandy hit New York City in the fall of 2012.
It cut down trees and power lines and brought strong winds and floods.
New Yorkers had to deal with blackouts and brownouts.
It is thought no storm before has brought so much power loss to New York.
CR
CR
A large part of New York had no power after the storm hit the coast.
Blackouts in the city went from 14th Street to downtown.
The Upper West Side and Upper East Side did have power and internet.
But the scale of the storm was vast and had not been seen before.
CR
CR
CR
They didn't start to repair the damage to power and water for three days.
The storm was a test for the skills of repair workers.
It was cold, wet and no fun in New York City.
We couldn't cross the road because the light was red.
The internet has made talking to people across the world easy.
We laugh now about the tough times we have had in the past.
Applause means clapping, but a pause means to wait a while.
Autumn comes after August, and is the same as Fall.
To cut something in half means that each part is the same size.
If you hold your breath for too long it is bad for your health.
A glass of fruit juice is good for you.
A new idea is like a breath of fresh air.
People used to say that bread was the staff of life.
To dread a thing means to fear that something bad will happen.
Coughs and sneezes spread diseases.
CR
CR
A battle which took place nearly 2,500 years ago is still well known.
300 Spartans fought against the Persian army.
The Spartans had no fear of death and would not give in.
CR
In those days the Spartans lived a tough and simple life in the south of Greece.
The King of Persia had sent a huge army to attack Greece.
While the other Greeks talked about how to fight the Persians, the 300 Spartans set off.
They were quick to cross the rough hill country and stop the Persians at a narrow pass.
The Persian army had to pause.
CR
CR
Then came the hard fighting in which the Spartans caused a lot of slaughter.
But in the end neither their skill nor their courage was enough.
They could not hold back the Persians.
CR
CR
When the Persians broke through the pass, they found they had lost many men.
The other Greeks no longer just talked but were ready to fight.
The 300 brave Spartans had shown them that they could fight the Persians and win.
"We the people..." is at the start of the Constitution of the United States.
The war made foreign countries choose if they were our friends or foes.
CR
CR
CR
Each state has a simple saying which is its motto.
The motto for North Carolina is: "To be, rather than to seem".
CR
CR
There was plenty for the spy to observe at the foreign army base.
She had to note the objects which looked like missiles and she had to count the trucks.
She had to remember all this and more for her report.
She had to get enough to obstruct the attack.
CR
On January 18, 1803, President Thomas Jefferson got Congress to pay for a group of explorers to travel to the Pacific Ocean.
The United States had bought a huge plot of land from France.
That land was called the Louisiana Purchase.
President Jefferson had to have someone map and explore the new land and report back to Congress.
CR
Lewis and Clark began their trip from St Louis, Missouri.
Their lengthy trip took two years, four months and nine days.
Slowly, they took note of all the rivers, mountains, deserts and passes.
They found plenty to observe and marvel at.
On November 7, 1805, Lewis and Clark and their group got to the west coast.
CR
The result was these men were the first to map the land that would become the United States.
They made maps and notes which helped to chart our country.
People today say the Lewis and Clark trip was one of the most important events in American history.
A shrub is like a small tree or bush.
Lucas was a very shrewd person, who knew better than to get into a fight.
After a lengthy meeting, the people who wanted to obstruct it gave in and left for home.
CR
The Redcoat meant to throw the colonist out of his way.
John took three quick strides and grabbed his arm just in time.
There was a struggle and the man thrust John away.
John ran after him to the corner of the lane, but all he could see was a stretch of empty road.
John went straight back to the house in case the British were to come again.
CR
The boys had fallen through the ice into the lake.
A young woman thought very quickly and brought a plank to place across the ice.
CR
CR
Her friend threw a rope which one of the boys caught right away.
Although it was quite a strain, they got the boys out safely.
CR
Though their teeth were chattering with the cold, they were happy when they were brought home to sit by the huge fire.
They had cookies and warm milk.
CR
CR
It taught the boys that the weight of two of them together was too much for the thin ice.
CR
We do not often think about skin.
Skin is one of the most amazing things in life.
CR
CR
Skin is alive.
It sends signals to the brain.
It takes in light which helps to keep the body fit.
That is why we like the sun.
CR
CR
The skin is elastic so it can stretch.
It is also waterproof, so that when we have a bath, the water does not get in.
Yet it is not waterproof the other way, because it will let water out when we get hot.
In the same way, it keeps dirt out.
CR
CR
Our skin is really many skins, one on top of the other.
CR
If we get a scrape, the outer skin may be scraped off.
CR
Under it is an inner skin, which is pink and hurts if it is touched.
CR
Doug turned the screw to make it tighter but that just caused the wood to split.
He went to swap the piece of wood for a fresh piece.
Then he got a splinter in his thumb.
He pulled the splinter out with a pair of pliers before boring a fresh hole.
The new screw fit in very nicely.
He went to show the completed job to his boss.
CR
CR
“Great job, Doug,” said his boss.
CR
CR
He didn’t know about the wood which the screw had split, or about the splinter which Doug had got in his thumb.
CR
CR
Many places are important in the American War of Independence but there is one place that tested the spirit of the new country: Valley Forge.
More than 2,000 soldiers of the army died there of hunger, illness and the cold in the winter of 1777-1778.
In December 1777, General Washington needed to find a place his small army could use as a camp for the winter.
He chose Valley Forge.
Soon after they got there, the winter became very harsh and very cold.
The army had to split logs to make cabins while the snow was falling.
Many of the soldiers had no shoes.
Most had no winter coats.
All suffered from a lack of food.
Several illnesses spread through the camp.
Most of the soldiers became sick and many of them died.
CR
CR
CR
General Washington wrote letters to Congress asking for help.
The army had to get stronger or it would lose the war.
But Congress was slow to give aid.
CR
As time passed, the army changed.
A pact with France brought some help.
The army left in 1778 with a power that sprang from hardship.
The men who lived through that bitter winter became well-trained soldiers who no longer feared the British army.
The thief sprang up and began to sprint across the field.
If you think I believe that, you are making a great mistake.
An old proverb says it is better to give than to receive.
When a fly walks on the ceiling, does it think we're upside down?
To sprain your ankle is bad enough, but it is worse if you break it.
Ask for a receipt when you pay for it.
CR
The weird thing about rock climbing is that the harder it is the more you like it.
CR
CR
You can climb simple rocks or try more advanced cliffs, boulders and towers.
You will need things to help your climb, like bolts, for support.
Or you can use chocks, stoppers, nuts and cramming devices.
These come out and do not damage the rock.
CR
CR
CR
No matter what helped you get up, and how hard you pushed yourself, in the end you will feel a fierce thrill from what you have done.
CR
The Civil War was fought from 1861 to1865.
A civil war is when people from the same country fight each other.
It began when the Southern states felt forced to set up on their own.
This was because of genuine splits with the North over state rights, tax and slavery.
This war brought the US to the edge of breaking up.
The death toll is thought to be 750,000 soldiers from both sides.
CR
CR
After four years of fierce combat, the North won and slavery was outlawed.
The split between North and South had passed.
In general, the soldiers went back to their homes and picked up their lives.
Both sides found the will to heal the wounds of the nation.
CR
CR
When we study geography we generally read books about the different regions of the world.
CR
CR
But if we travel, geography comes to life as something wonderful and new for us.
CR
CR
The engines of our ships and airplanes can take us where it seems no one else has been before.
CR
CR
As we look on great forests and giant mountain peaks, we feel we must be the first to ever see them.
Jordan suspected that her friend would try to change the subject.
It takes a lot of practice for a tennis player to become an expert.
The policeman inspected the car carefully.
The driver had neglected to check his lights.
The company expected news from the bank that day.
The children in the play acted their parts very well.
CR
Carrie's friend, Steve, lent her his car for the weekend.
Carrie had not expected this, but she could not object to such a friendly act.
CR
CR
However, Steve neglected to mention that the gas would show a full tank all the time.
Nor did he give Carrie the information that the engine would sometimes give a loud bang.
CR
CR
Carrie had an awful time that weekend.
She was careful in any conversation with Steve after that.
She was scared that Steve would offer her the car again.
CR
If cars were mentioned, Carrie would change the subject quickly.
CR
CR
The bald eagle is native to the US and stands for the country.
We can see the bald eagle on the seal of the US, and on logos, coins, postage stamps and other things.
On the Great Seal of the United States, it is shown holding 13 arrows and an olive branch.
CR
CR
The bald eagle is also important for the Native peoples.
It is sacred to some of the tribes and its feathers are used in many ways.
CR
CR
It stands for many positive aspects of American culture.
